info.title: FaxCore Web API
info.description: API documentation for FaxCore. Extended REST based SDK. All request and response in JSON format only.
info.termsOfService: The use of this API is bound by the terms and conditions set upon by FaxCore. FaxCore is not responsible for the usage of this service. This service is provided as is and does not cover under standard maintenance agreement.

post.summary: Create new user in a specific domain. Only create in domain which user belong to.

delete.summary: Delete a specific user. Roles segregation applies.

post.summary: Move user to specific domain.
Activate user in a specific domain. Only applicable to users in domain which the user has access to.
Deactivate user in a specific domain. Only applicable to users in domain which the user has access to.
Create new inbound routing rules in FaxCore.
Disable existing inbound routing rules..
Enable existing inbound routing rules..
Delete existing inbound routing rules..

put.summary: Update tracking record for a specific message.

post.summary: Retrieve tracking record for a specific message. Roles segregation applies.
Retrieve authenticated user’s details.
Retrieve specific user’s fax settings.
Retrieve user list in a domain. Roles segregation applies.
Retrieve authenticated user’s details.
Search for inbound routes with user input filters.

get.summary: List all available domain. Roles segregation applies.

post.summary: Create a new domain. Always specify parent domain.
Create new user address book. Roles segregation applies.

delete.summary: Delete specific address book from user account. Address book with existing contact will get exception.

post.summary: Update existing user address book. Roles segregation applies.
List all user's personal address book. Exclude shared address books.
Create new contact in specific address book. Payload contain only 1 address for each type.

delete.summary: Delete specific contact from user address book.

post.summary: Update existing contact in specific address book. Payload contain only 1 address for each type.
List all contacts in a specific address book belong to user.
Create new message (fax and email). The usual user permissions applies.
Create new message (internal). The usual user permissions applies.
Cancel message active transmission.

delete.summary: Delete messages belong to authenticated user. Only able to delete own messages. For SystemAdmin, able to delete all messages. Delete one or many messages.

post.summary: Send message on behalf of other user.

Download message based on filter values. This consolidate 4 different methods in ev5 into single entry. Delivery ID(transaction id) in ev5 no longer relevant as transaction ID value only available from get message details.
Forward specific message to list of users.
Retrieve message details belong to auth user. SystemAdmin and DomainAdmin role able to access messages according to standard role segregation.
Retrieve user's message list from specified folder.
Retrieve message status.
Retrieve list of folders belong to user. This will include private folder but not delegation. Role and domain segregation applies.
Retrieve delete status for a specific message. Roles segregation applies.
Retrieve message delete status. This is a simple message delete flag retrieval.
Mark specific message read status to read or unread. This is a simple message flag set.
Mark specific message download flag to true or false.
Move existing message from one folder to another, include personal folder. The move only happen within user's account, does not support cross user account.
Set existing message with failed status to resend (ie, reattempt.). Only valid for own messages.
Upload files to retrieve encrypted file name to pass in to message send

get.summary: List all user's cover page. (Includes shared documents, domain documents)

post.summary: List all user's document folders
List all user's documents.
Create a document.
To approve or disapprove a message.
Outbound and inbound message can assign to any users within the same domain.
Search Messages within the domain
